text.title: ES Agentic Pipeline Failure Modes
text.summary: Four canonical regressions when AI agents scaffold event-sourcing code. Each pairs symptom + root cause + fix.

antipattern.symptom: Agent emits `UserUpdated`, `OrderUpdated`, `OrderUpdated_v2`. Event payloads are flat state snapshots; no intent encoded; downstream projections cannot tell what changed.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent saw "event sourcing" + "update" together in tutorials.
antipattern.fix: Reject. Re-prompt with the past-tense rule + 3 worked examples (`OrderPlaced`, `ItemAdded`, `PaymentCaptured`). Provide the aggregate's domain-event catalog as context.

antipattern.symptom: Command method body: `self.status = "placed"; self._events.append(OrderPlaced(...))`. Replay diverges from live execution because `apply` doesn't set `status`.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent treats command method as a setter + side-effect emitter.
antipattern.fix: Reject. Re-prompt with the aggregate-apply contract: command methods validate + emit; apply handlers mutate.

antipattern.symptom: Generated code calls `event_store.append_one(stream, evt)` — the real library is `client.append(stream_id, events)`. CI build fails with `AttributeError`.

root-cause.source: LLM hallucination + missing library docs in context

antipattern.root-cause: Agent reused symbols from a different event-store library.
antipattern.fix: Reject. Provide the library's relevant docstrings inline in the prompt. Add a CI check that resolves imports before merge.

antipattern.symptom: Projection handler: `if order.total > 1000: send_email(order.customer)`. Rebuild-from-offset-0 fires every email again.
antipattern.fix: Reject. Move side effect to a process manager / saga subscribed to events. Projection only writes to read store.
